what_it_does: Defines a self-contained React widget workshop for ConceptV that can create, preview, edit, vary, refine with Vi, and export dashboard, hub, and embeddable widgets across KPI, media, and hybrid systems.
observations: Declares built-in widget kinds, widget systems, mock KPI feeds, and sparkline datasets in the same file as the editor and renderer.
Uses browser globals for React hooks, CanvasHeader, ViShape, WSCandidateGallery, WS_AI, claude, toast helpers, and Type Registry promotion flows.
Renders the widget itself separately from contextual shells for dashboard, Virtual Hub, and partner embed placements.
Includes AI-assisted drafting, alternative candidate generation, targeted refinement, mocked live data motion, variation previews, and JSX/PNG/PDF export controls.
Maintains widget state through saveDoc callbacks rather than local document ownership.
standouts: Combines schema defaults, presentational widget rendering, editing controls, contextual preview frames, Vi generation, and export logic in one file.
Treats widget kind and content system as separate axes, allowing the same data to be shown across multiple surfaces and layouts.
Includes a generated JSX export path that serializes the current widget data into a standalone React component string.
conventions: Uses function components and small helper functions rather than classes.
Aliases React hooks with file-specific suffixes.
Uses inline styles extensively for widget internals and CSS classes for workshop layout shells.
Stores editor-facing updates as shallow document patches and nested data patches.
Exposes several functions and constants on window instead of using ES module exports.
concerns: Many dependencies are implicit window globals, so this file depends on runtime setup not visible in the file.
Several nested editor update helpers assume data.kpis or data.rows exists before mapping or filtering.
viDraftWidget accepts loosely parsed AI JSON and applies limited schema validation before constructing widget data.
widgetToJSX directly uses WIDGET_KINDS.find(...).size and could fail for an unknown widgetKind.
The PNG export path depends on serialized foreignObject behavior and the file comment notes browser and permission limitations.
A large amount of rendering, editor state, AI orchestration, and export behavior lives in one file.
notes: This file is the local widget workshop surface: it defines widget defaults, renders the widget, edits its data, previews it in target contexts, invokes Vi, and exports artifacts.
questions: What runtime guarantees provide window.claude, window.WS_AI, CanvasHeader, ViShape, and WSCandidateGallery?
Should user-defined widget kinds and systems be supported everywhere, including export sizing and variation previews?
Is generated JSX intended as production-ready output or a handoff scaffold?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: the file is a self-contained editor for KPI, media, and hybrid ConceptV widgets across dashboard, hub, and embeddable contexts.
intention_signals: The file appears intended to let users rapidly draft a branded property widget, inspect it in realistic placement contexts, adjust content and system choices, generate alternatives with Vi, and hand off the result as a template, Type, image, PDF, or JSX.
novelty: The distinct element is the combination of AI-guided widget drafting, live mocked data, context frames, and export generation inside a single browser-local workshop file.
contribution: Contributes a compact widget-building substrate: built-in widget schemas, a reusable renderer for KPI/media/hybrid cards, context-aware preview frames, editor controls, Vi-driven generation hooks, and artifact export helpers that a fused system would keep as widget authoring and handoff capabilities.
summary_for_embedding: A React browser file that defines ConceptV widget defaults, AI drafting, KPI/media/hybrid rendering, dashboard/hub/embed preview contexts, editing panels, variation controls, and JSX/PNG/PDF export.
intention_for_embedding: This was for creating and refining ConceptV widgets in Workshop with mocked data, Vi assistance, realistic placement previews, and reusable handoff outputs.

symbols.does: Creates a default widget document for a given kind and system.
symbols.description: This function fills a widget with default metadata, KPI rows, media placeholder data, sparkline data, CTA content, and flags for mock mode and branding. It falls back to dashboard and KPI when inputs are not provided.

symbols.does: Returns a default title for a widget system.
symbols.description: This helper maps kpi to Performance, media to Tower East, and other systems to Tower East · live. It is used when creating blank widgets and when deciding whether a refinement should replace a default title.

symbols.does: Asks Vi to draft a widget from a brief and returns a normalized widget document.
symbols.description: This async function builds a detailed JSON-only prompt for window.claude.complete, parses the response, resolves kind and system, converts chart trend names into point arrays, and clamps KPI and row counts. It preserves the file's widget document shape while treating AI output as draft data.

symbols.does: Checks whether a kind or system input should be treated as unspecified.
symbols.description: This local helper inside viDraftWidget returns true for empty values and the string auto. It allows the prompt to either lock a choice or let Vi choose within the provided enum descriptions.

symbols.does: Renders the widget card itself from a widget document.
symbols.description: This presentational component chooses size and accent colors from widget kind and co-brand state, renders the header, delegates the body to KPI, media, or hybrid renderers, and conditionally shows the CTA. It is used by the stage, variation thumbnails, and exported visual preview paths.

symbols.does: Renders KPI tiles and an optional sparkline for KPI widgets.
symbols.description: This component lays out up to three KPI tiles in a grid and adds a sparkline when chart points are present. It receives accent color from the parent renderer.

symbols.does: Renders an imagery-led widget body with optional single KPI support.
symbols.description: This component displays a stylized media placeholder with caption text and, when available, a single KPI row with value, label, and delta. The media block uses accent-soft styling and patterned background treatment.

symbols.does: Renders a mixed widget body with KPIs, rows, and optional media area.
symbols.description: This component combines two compact KPI tiles with a short row table, and for non-dashboard widgets adds a right-side media placeholder. Its layout changes based on widgetKind so dashboard widgets stay single-column.

symbols.does: Displays one KPI value, label, and optional delta.
symbols.description: This small card-like component formats KPI content with compact and regular sizing variants. It uses the shared Delta component for directional movement text.

symbols.does: Displays a directional delta indicator.
symbols.description: This component maps deltaKind to success, error, or muted color and uses an up arrow, down arrow, or dot marker. It supports compact typography for dense KPI tiles.

symbols.does: Displays a colored status marker for table rows.
symbols.description: This component maps ok, warn, and info status values to status color variables, falling back to muted foreground. It renders only a circular dot without text.

symbols.does: Renders an SVG sparkline with area fill and endpoint marker.
symbols.description: This component normalizes numeric points into a fixed viewBox, builds path and area strings, and displays an optional uppercase label. It returns null when there are no points.

symbols.does: Provides the main widget editor workspace.
symbols.description: This component coordinates document editing, live preview state, export menu visibility, variation panel visibility, and AI candidate gallery state. It renders the workshop header, kind/system controls, data panel, stage preview, optional variations panel, and candidate gallery.

symbols.does: Saves a shallow patch onto the current widget document.
symbols.description: This local helper merges a patch into doc and passes the result to saveDoc. It is the general update path for top-level widget document fields.

symbols.does: Saves a shallow patch onto the widget data object.
symbols.description: This local helper merges a patch into doc.data while preserving other top-level document fields. It is passed down to the data panel for content edits.

symbols.does: Updates the widgetKind field.
symbols.description: This local helper wraps update with a widgetKind patch. It is used by the kind picker controls.

symbols.does: Updates the system field.
symbols.description: This local helper wraps update with a system patch. It is used by the system picker controls.

symbols.does: Generates candidate alternatives for a target and opens the candidate gallery.
symbols.description: This async local helper initializes gallery state as busy, calls window.WS_AI.generateCandidates with the current doc and target, then stores returned candidates. On failure it closes the gallery and shows a toast.

symbols.does: Regenerates gallery candidates using a refinement hint.
symbols.description: This async local helper keeps the current gallery target, marks the gallery busy, and requests a new candidate set from window.WS_AI.generateCandidates with the hint as brief. Failure clears only the busy state.

symbols.does: Applies a selected AI candidate and closes the gallery.
symbols.description: This local helper either invokes a custom pick override or applies a candidate diff through window.WS_AI.applyDiff. It then clears gallery state and emits an Applied toast.

symbols.does: Renders the widget preview inside its contextual frame.
symbols.description: This component manages hover state and a timer-driven mocked data tick. When mocked mode is active, it nudges numeric KPI values and rotates sparkline trends before passing the document to WidgetRender.

symbols.does: Wraps a widget preview in dashboard, hub, or embed context chrome.
symbols.description: This component renders different surrounding UI mockups for dashboard, Virtual Hub, and partner embed placements. It places children into a target slot and labels the context beneath the frame.

symbols.does: Renders the editable content controls for a widget document.
symbols.description: This component exposes fields for eyebrow, title, KPIs, sparkline, media caption, rows, CTA, and Vi refinement based on the active system. It also includes buttons for AI-generated alternatives for content and KPI sets.

symbols.does: Renders a compact button for requesting Vi alternatives.
symbols.description: This local component inside WidgetDataPanel displays a Vi icon and alts label with a provided title and click handler. It is used near content and KPI section headings.

symbols.does: Updates one KPI entry by index.
symbols.description: This local helper maps over data.kpis and merges a patch into the matching KPI. It writes the updated array through updateData.

symbols.does: Removes one KPI entry by index.
symbols.description: This local helper filters data.kpis to exclude the selected index. It writes the shortened array through updateData.

symbols.does: Appends a default KPI entry.
symbols.description: This local helper adds a KPI labeled New with value 0, flat delta, and +0 delta text. It tolerates a missing kpis array by spreading an empty fallback.

symbols.does: Updates one table row by index.
symbols.description: This local helper maps over data.rows and merges a patch into the matching row. It writes the updated row array through updateData.

symbols.does: Removes one table row by index.
symbols.description: This local helper filters data.rows to exclude the selected index. It writes the shortened row array through updateData.

symbols.does: Appends a default table row.
symbols.description: This local helper adds a row labeled Item with value 0 and ok status. It tolerates a missing rows array by spreading an empty fallback.

symbols.does: Infers the named sparkline trend from a points array.
symbols.description: This helper compares the JSON string form of the provided points with each SPARK_DATA entry. It returns rising when no match is found.

symbols.does: Wraps form controls with a labeled field structure.
symbols.description: This small component renders a label element with a text span and child controls. It standardizes simple field layout in the data panel.

symbols.does: Renders a text box for asking Vi to refine the widget.
symbols.description: This component keeps local prompt and busy state, accepts short natural-language instructions, and calls the local go function on Refine. It is embedded in the Vi section of the data panel.

symbols.does: Runs a Vi refinement request from the refine box.
symbols.description: This async local helper exits when the prompt is empty or already busy, calls viDraftWidget with the current kind and system, and updates the document data with the returned draft. It keeps a customized title when the existing title differs from the system default.

symbols.does: Displays selectable previews for alternate systems and widget kinds.
symbols.description: This component renders thumbnail buttons for KPI, media, and hybrid system swaps, then separate buttons for dashboard, hub, and embed kind swaps. It calls onPick with a patch for the selected axis.

symbols.does: Provides export actions for the current widget.
symbols.description: This component computes JSX output with widgetToJSX, lets the user preview the JSX string, and offers download JSX, PNG export, and print-to-PDF actions. It owns the local export view state.

symbols.does: Downloads the generated JSX as a text file.
symbols.description: This local helper creates a text Blob from the generated code, builds an object URL, clicks a temporary anchor with a slugified filename, revokes the URL, and closes the export menu.

symbols.does: Attempts to export the rendered widget frame as a PNG.
symbols.description: This async local helper selects the first .ws-widget-frame node, serializes a clone into an SVG foreignObject, draws it onto a high-resolution canvas, and downloads the resulting PNG blob. It shows toast messages when the node is missing or export fails.

symbols.does: Triggers browser printing for PDF output.
symbols.description: This local helper calls window.print and then closes the export menu. It relies on browser print behavior and any surrounding print styles.

symbols.does: Returns a cloned node for export serialization.
symbols.description: This helper currently performs a minimal cloneNode(true) operation. The adjacent comment notes that the SVG foreignObject path may not work across browsers and that JSX and print are alternatives.

symbols.does: Serializes the current widget document into a standalone React component string.
symbols.description: This helper embeds widget data as JSON, sets dimensions from the built-in kind definition, and conditionally includes KPI and CTA sections in the returned JSX text. It uses pascalCase for the component name and produces a compact handoff component rather than the full in-editor renderer.

symbols.does: Names the generated exported React component in the widgetToJSX template.
symbols.description: This generated symbol represents the default function declaration interpolated into the JSX string using pascalCase(doc.title). It is not executed in this file; it is emitted as text for downloaded or previewed React output.

symbols.does: Converts a string into a PascalCase identifier-like name.
symbols.description: This helper removes non-alphanumeric separators, title-cases each word, joins them, and falls back to Widget. It is used for generated React component names.

symbols.does: Converts a string into a lowercase dash-separated slug.
symbols.description: This helper lowercases text, replaces non-alphanumeric runs with hyphens, trims edge hyphens, and falls back to widget. It is used for exported filenames.
